evidence: get_running_config (R2): GigabitEthernet0/0/0 configured 172.16.0.1/24 (shows 172.16.0.0/24 is the LAN where 172.16.0.12 would reside)
alert_description: 25 ICMP attempts to 172.16.0.12, 0 replies (no responses observed)

missing_info: Is 172.16.0.12 statically configured on the server (or otherwise assigned) or expected to obtain its address via DHCP?
Is the server powered on and physically connected to R2's LAN (any link/port status or switchport show)?
Does R2 have an ARP entry for 172.16.0.12 (show ip arp 172.16.0.12)?
From which device/IP were the 25 ICMP attempts sourced (to confirm path and source reachability)?
Are there host-based firewall rules on the server blocking ICMP?

plan_history.intent_description: Host 172.16.0.12 is unreachable (25 ICMP attempts, 0 replies). R2's DHCP configuration currently excludes the entire 172.16.0.0/24, likely preventing the server from obtaining an IP. Goal: restore reachability by allowing 172.16.0.12 to obtain an address (remove exclusion) or create a static binding.
plan_history.problem: R2 DHCP excluded-address list contains low=172.16.0.1 high=172.16.0.254 (full /24 excluded), preventing DHCP assignment in 172.16.0.0/24; server 172.16.0.12 has no responses to ICMP.
plan_history.fix_summary: On router2 remove the DHCP excluded-address range 172.16.0.1–172.16.0.254 so the DHCP pool for 172.16.0.0/24 can assign addresses. Assumption: 172.16.0.12 is expected to use DHCP. Operational risk: devices previously excluded may receive addresses; schedule window if needed.

remaining_issues: Confirm whether 172.16.0.12 obtained a DHCP lease (or is statically configured) — check the server IP and R2 DHCP bindings (show ip dhcp binding).
Verify reachability to 172.16.0.12 (ping from R2 or another LAN host) and check R2 ARP table for 172.16.0.12 (show ip arp 172.16.0.12).
Verify the server is powered on and physically connected to the R2 LAN (check switchport/link status).
Check host-based firewall settings on 172.16.0.12 that may block ICMP.
